course_title: Impact Academy 2: Designing Solutions that Create Value
course_description:
In this course, students will work on a real project they have identified based on either a business idea or an industry partner’s business problem. Using humble enquiry, ethnography and cutting-edge data analysis and visualisation tools, students will rapidly identify, analyse, and describe targeted problems, value creation opportunities and solutions for stakeholders. Students design prototype solutions, business models and an execution plan with the goal of creating a desirable, feasible and viable strategy.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

The weighting of the final assignment has been increased to reflect the work involved in completing this assessment.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

Additional timetable information
Please note: Teaching staff do not have access to the timetabling system to help with class allocation. Therefore, should you need help with your timetable and/or allocation of classes, please email business.mytimetable@uq.edu.au from your UQ student email account with the following details:
Full Name
Student ID
Course Code
course_aims: Applying the cognitive empathy and entrepreneurial mindset developed in MGTS7820 Impact Academy 1, students will sharpen their capacity to discover and design new solutions by addressing unresolved organisational problems and/or exploiting unrealised opportunities within an organisation or sector of their choosing.

learning_outcomes.description: Working individually or in teams, identify and critically evaluate the desirability, feasibility, and viability of a value creation opportunity by applying concepts, tools, and techniques and gathering supporting data.
Critically evaluate one’s own suitability and willingness to execute a value creation opportunity in light of new insights and data sensemaking.
Convey cogent value creation strategies developed through synthesis of and adaptation to stakeholder feedback and insights.

assessment_summary.title: Opportunity Journey – Journal Reflection
assessment_summary.due_date: Entry reflection. 11/03/2026 1:00 pm; Progress reflection. 25/03/2026 1:00 pm; Progress reflection. 15/04/2026 1:00 pm; Progress reflection. 13/05/2026 1:00 pm; Exit reflection. 3/06/2026 1:00 pm

assessment_details.title: Opportunity Analysis – Idea Presentation
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Work integrated learning, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: You will pitch your idea (max 15 min, followed by Q&A) by analysing it against the backdrop of the concepts, tools, and techniques from the course including any initial validation and supporting evidence. Each student will present in class (given confidentially permits) and will respond to questions from the audience. Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy. AI Statement This assessment task is to be completed in-person. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
One member per team has to submit the slides via Blackboard BEFORE the pitch.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Please contact teaching staff directly if you are unable to attend or submit your pitch. An extension may be available for the submitted material only.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
10% Late Penalty applies to submitted material only. Late submissions are not accepted for in-class presentations. Failure to present at the scheduled time will result in a mark of zero for the presentation portion of this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Opportunity Journey – Journal Reflection
assessment_details.due_date: Entry reflection. 11/03/2026 1:00 pm Progress reflection. 25/03/2026 1:00 pm Progress reflection. 15/04/2026 1:00 pm Progress reflection. 13/05/2026 1:00 pm Exit reflection. 3/06/2026 1:00 pm
assessment_details.task_description: Throughout the course, you will keep a reflective journal of your opportunity journey. Five times throughout the course, you will reflect on the progress that you make and the successes and challenges you faced while developing your opportunities based on the concepts, tools, and techniques from the course. Individual reflections do not need to (but can) be longer than 200 words / 2–3 minutes video and include entry and exit reflections: 1. What new aspects did you add to your opportunity, and why? 2. What aspect of your opportunity did you abandon, and why? 3. What existing aspects of your opportunity will you double down on, and why? AI Statement Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Please submit your reflections individually via Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Opportunity and Execution Presentation
assessment_details.task_description: You will deliver a max 7 min investor pitch in video format that demonstrates the opportunity is viable, feasible, and desirable. The video is accompanied by an explanation summarising the logic and evidence underlying the proposed solution and how it was informed by the insights gained throughout the course. Submission of the explanation can be in video (20-30 minutes with slide deck) or written format (20 pages). AI Statement This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. learning_activities.topic: Day 1 - Saturday 28/02/2026 - From ideas to opportunities Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Day 2 - Saturday - 14/03/2026 - From opportunities to value propositions Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Day 3 - Saturday - 28/03/2026 - From value propositions to business models Learning outcomes: L01, L03
Day 4 - Saturday - 18/04/2026 - From business models to MVPs to going to market Learning outcomes: L01, L03
Day 5 - Saturday - 02/05/2026 - Developing the startup pitch Learning outcomes: L01, L03
Day 6 - Saturday - 16/05/2026 - Scaling, unlocking resources & avoiding Pitfalls Learning outcomes: L01, L02

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Work Integrated Learning and Work Experience Policy Learning activities
